prompt: Add tests for the HTTP request parser covering GET, POST with body, headers, and malformed requests

turns.content:
<task>
Add tests for the HTTP request parser covering GET, POST with body, headers, and malformed requests
</task>

1: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
2: from typing import Dict, Optional
3: 
4: 
5: @dataclass
6: class HttpRequest:
7:     method: str
8:     path: str
9:     version: str = "HTTP/1.1"
10:     headers: Dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)
11:     body: str = ""
12: 
13: 
14: class ParseError(Exception):
15:     pass
16: 
17: 
18: def parse_request(raw: str) -> HttpRequest:
19:     """Parse raw HTTP request string."""
20:     parts = raw.split("\r\n\r\n", 1)
21:     header_section = parts[0]
22:     body = parts[1] if len(parts) > 1 else ""
23: 
24:     lines = header_section.split("\r\n")
25:     if not lines:
26:         raise ParseError("Empty request")
27: 
28:     request_line = lines[0].split(" ")
29:     if len(request_line) < 3:
30:         raise ParseError(f"Invalid request line: {lines[0]}")
31: 
32:     method, path, version = request_line[0], request_line[1], request_line[2]
33:     headers = {}
34:     for line in lines[1:]:
35:         if ": " in line:
36:             key, value = line.split(": ", 1)
37:             headers[key] = value
38: 
39:     return HttpRequest(method=method, path=path, version=version, headers=headers, body=body)

arguments.summary: Read HTTP parser with HttpRequest dataclass and parse_request function. Handles method/path/version, headers, body (split on blank line). ParseError for malformed. Need tests.

next_steps.description: Create tests/test_parser.py with tests for GET request, POST with body, multiple headers, malformed request line, empty input

arguments.content:
import pytest
from httpparser.parser import parse_request, ParseError


def test_get_request():
    raw = "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: example.com\r\n\r\n"
    req = parse_request(raw)
    assert req.method == "GET"
    assert req.path == "/index.html"
    assert req.version == "HTTP/1.1"
    assert req.headers["Host"] == "example.com"
    assert req.body == ""


def test_post_with_body():
    raw = (
        "POST /api/data HTTP/1.1\r\n"
        "Content-Type: application/json\r\n"
        "Content-Length: 13\r\n"
        "\r\n"
        '{"key": "val"}'
    )
    req = parse_request(raw)
    assert req.method == "POST"
    assert req.path == "/api/data"
    assert req.headers["Content-Type"] == "application/json"
    assert req.body == '{"key": "val"}'


def test_multiple_headers():
    raw = (
        "GET / HTTP/1.1\r\n"
        "Host: example.com\r\n"
        "Accept: text/html\r\n"
        "User-Agent: TestBot/1.0\r\n"
        "\r\n"
    )
    req = parse_request(raw)
    assert len(req.headers) == 3
    assert req.headers["Accept"] == "text/html"


def test_malformed_request_line():
    with pytest.raises(ParseError, match="Invalid request line"):
        parse_request("INVALID\r\n\r\n")


def test_empty_request():
    with pytest.raises(ParseError):
        parse_request("\r\n\r\n")
